title: Accessibility/legibility evidence brief (low-vision + honest on contested dyslexia-font claims)
context: open-accessible-fonts will produce an accessibility-first Latin family. Before any design work begins, the project needs a clear, honest evidence brief that separates what published research actually supports from contested or unsupported claims — particularly for dyslexia-specific font interventions, where the literature is mixed. This brief drives the M1 design brief and establishes the non-medical framing used in all project artifacts.
objective: Summarize published legibility evidence for low-vision and dyslexic readers, honestly characterizing contested findings, derive the accessibility-critical glyph-differentiation set, and define the standard non-medical framing text for all project artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: Brief summarizes published evidence on legibility for low-vision readers (macular degeneration, cataract, low acuity), covering letter differentiation, x-height, spacing, contrast, and aperture.

Brief honestly characterizes the contested, mixed evidence on dyslexia-specific fonts: cites both supporting and null/contrary findings; explicitly does not over-claim; does not assert clinical efficacy.

Brief defines the standard non-medical framing text to be reused verbatim in README, specimens, and usage guides: 'designed using published legibility research; not a treatment, not a diagnosis, not a medical device.'
Brief lists the accessibility-critical glyph-differentiation set (I/l/1, O/0, b/d/p/q, rn/m, and related confusable pairs) to drive the M1 design brief.

Reviewer sign-off from an accessibility/vision specialist is recorded as required before the brief is used to inform medical or efficacy framing; without this sign-off, the brief is marked as pending specialist review.
Document is CC-BY-4.0; all cited works are referenced with sufficient detail to locate; no identifiable human-subjects data included.
resources: PLAN.md (section 3: goals — honesty on dyslexia font claims)
output: A published evidence brief (CC-BY-4.0) covering low-vision and dyslexia legibility research, including the standard non-medical framing text, the accessibility-critical differentiation glyph set, and an honest summary of contested claims, merged into the repository documentation.
